_comment: DO NOT EDIT: This file was auto-generated by Pterodactyl Egg Exporter
name: WhatsApp Baileys Bot
author: your@email.com
description: A lightweight WhatsApp bot built with Baileys. Scan the QR code in the console to connect. Settings and custom commands are saved in data/settings.json.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# WhatsApp Baileys Bot - Pterodactyl Install Script
apt-get update -y
apt-get install -y git curl

# Install Node.js 22 if not already present
if ! command -v node &> /dev/null; then
  curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash -
  apt-get install -y nodejs
fi

node -v
npm -v

# Install dependencies
cd /home/container
npm install

echo "Installation complete! Start the server to get the QR code."

variables.name: Phone Number
variables.description: Your WhatsApp phone number in full international format, digits only. Example: 628123456789 (no +, spaces, or dashes). This is used to generate the pairing code shown in the console on first start.

variables.name: Log Level
variables.description: Pino log level. Options: trace, debug, info, warn, error. Default: info

variables.name: Node Version
variables.description: The node version to use. Do not change this unless you know what you are doing.
